124530.explanation: The council is the people's representation. In several dossiers, participation is also applied.

124533.explanation: The SGP is basically positive towards New Economic Drivers in rural areas, when fitting within policy and environment.

124534.explanation: The municipality can play a stimulating/driving role in sustainability initiatives, including financially; initiatives must be able to support themselves in the long term.

124536.explanation: Tholen must meet its obligations under the Distribution Act. We make arrangements regarding implementation in Zeeland context, with municipalities and province, and with the COA.

124541.explanation: The municipality can guide initiatives from the villages but does not take the lead in organizing a fireworks show.

124542.explanation: The recycling center functions well. Besides, we do not want bulky waste dumped on the street.

124544.explanation: Entrepreneurs are primarily responsible themselves for the accessibility of their buildings. For municipal buildings, we apply accessibility measures when necessary and possible.

124545.explanation: Development should connect as much as possible to the towns to preserve the openness of the Tholen landscape, in accordance with the Spatial Vision.

124546.explanation: The municipality offers a contribution towards the costs, but does not pay the full membership fee.

124548.explanation: The municipality facilitates and supports music education in Tholen, but a financial contribution from parents is expected. For children from low-income families, the municipality provides a cost allowance.

124551.explanation: More enforcement officers (BOA's) is not an end in itself. The SGP primarily wants to strengthen police capacity.

124552.explanation: Cameras can be used in exceptional situations to enhance security, but are not an end in themselves. We are cautious with the privacy of residents.

124556.explanation: The current practice, in which permanent residence in holiday homes is not allowed, will remain in place as far as we are concerned.

124557.explanation: Expansion remains possible, but we also focus on restructuring within the towns. We will use the rural area sparingly.

124558.explanation: When possible, we find a social contribution in return no more than normal.

124560.explanation: The income threshold was raised some years ago with the support of the SGP to 130% of the social assistance standard.

124562.explanation: The municipality takes the lead in ensuring sufficient charging point capacity.

124563.explanation: The maximum speed is 30 km/h in many places, and 50 km/h where possible. That will remain so.

124564.explanation: Freedom of choice is an important principle for the SGP.

124565.explanation: Price, quality, and sufficient availability of faith-based care are all important to us-not just price.

124567.explanation: Existing industrial sites can be expanded, especially in Tholen and Sint-Philipsland; no new sites will be added.

124568.explanation: The current policy works well; Sunday rest is a great value and a Biblical principle, reason to maintain Sunday rest.

124574.explanation: Zeelandic is a beautiful dialect that parents should primarily pass on to their children themselves-in principle, education is not responsible for this. However, we do encourage primary schools to positively highlight the value of Zeelandic as collective heritage.

124575.explanation: Coffeeshops do not fit the DNA of Tholen nor the municipality's commitment to healthy and safe living and growing up.

124576.explanation: We pay attention to a balanced composition of housing types and segments, but are not fixated on a number. 30% is a target figure, not a must.

124577.explanation: We are not against high-rise buildings; scarcity of land makes high-rise a logical alternative to ground-bound housing. However, we believe high-rise must fit the surroundings. You will not often see 'tall residential towers' in Tholen in the future.

124578.explanation: Room-by-room rental is possible under conditions, but we want to prevent houses in towns from being rented out on a large scale to, for example, seasonal workers. Therefore, we are cautious with permits for room-by-room rental.

124579.explanation: Parking problems occur in every town. We are working to reduce parking pressure in the streets and focus on creating sufficient parking lots.

124581.explanation: The recent expansion of municipal responsibilities and the many societal challenges make expanding the executive board a logical option.

124583.explanation: More recreational facilities aimed at day-trippers and Tholen's own visitors fit with our focus on getting the basics in order: benches, trash cans, signage, routes, toilets.

124530.explanation: Not for nothing an elected representative body and sufficient moments for participation and involvement in processes.

124534.explanation: Facility support and sufficient administrative assistance for good initiatives from society

124536.explanation: Allowed but not necessary, also pending the implementation of the dispersion law.

124541.explanation: Nice idea but practically unworkable in a municipality like Tholen with 9 towns.

124544.explanation: Is the responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves; the municipality can facilitate and support with advice.

124545.explanation: Must fit within the spatial vision.

124546.explanation: If financially not possible, movement, sports, and social interaction are important.

124548.explanation: Music education is important for children.

124551.explanation: It turns out the community officers cannot handle it, and extra BOA capacity will help with this.

124552.explanation: Camera surveillance does not always contribute to the intended safety.

124556.explanation: As an absolute last resort in the case of significant housing shortages, for shorter periods and not instead of tourism.

124557.explanation: More building is necessary, also beyond existing village boundaries but connected to the towns and within existing frameworks.

124558.explanation: Provides advantage because you gain work experience, often come out of social isolation, and your self-esteem increases.

124562.explanation: The municipality should facilitate this on request from citizens and not initiate it themselves.

124563.explanation: Not throughout the entire built-up area; some roads are perfectly suitable for 50 km/h and traffic flow is important, also consider Public Transport.

124567.explanation: If there is demand from local entrepreneurs, and if it creates employment and other revenues.

124568.explanation: In some sectors it is possible and desirable; other entrepreneurs should have the free choice.

124574.explanation: Dialect can be learned at home, it does not need to be taught at schools, as it often causes disadvantages in further education.

124575.explanation: If an entrepreneur has a viable business case for this, complies with applicable laws and regulations, and submits an application to the Municipality.

124576.explanation: Build according to need, examined per village.

124577.explanation: If it fits within the spatial vision and can be integrated into the landscape.

124578.explanation: No municipal policy; a breeding ground for slumlords and deterioration of neighborhoods and surroundings, which does not benefit the livability of the villages.

124579.explanation: Some creativity is desired; participation with residents is important, but it should not dominate the streetscape, in combination with greenery, water, possibly covered with green roofs or solar panels.

124581.explanation: But a fourth Alderman does not necessarily mean 4 FTE (maintain balance).

124583.explanation: Existing policy, do what is possible and necessary.

124530.explanation: Not every topic is suitable for a referendum. When the general interest of Tholen is directly affected, a referendum can contribute to support and involvement, provided the outcome is carefully considered.

124533.explanation: Our party wants to strengthen the business continuity of farmers by encouraging more earning opportunities, e.g., through a farm shop, camping, or other small-scale side activities. This allows farmers to operate sustainably, create added value for their business, and support the local economy.

124534.explanation: Residents who generate sustainable energy strengthen their self-reliance. The municipality facilitates, does not structurally finance, with targeted support in cases of energy poverty or collective added value.

124536.explanation: Our party advocates for shelter that fits Tholen: small-scale, dispersed, and aligned with the carrying capacity of our villages. No large-scale locations that put pressure on livability. Decisions can only be made when preconditions and local facilities are in order.

124541.explanation: Firework displays are not a statutory duty; if desired, safety, environment, and minimal nuisance take priority.

124542.explanation: Residents can bring bulky waste themselves, supported by well-accessible and safe facilities provided by the municipality for a clean living environment.

124544.explanation: Accessibility is important for everyone. The municipality advises and facilitates entrepreneurs without financial contributions.

124545.explanation: Our party opposes farmers independently building homes on their own land outside existing villages. Spatial planning, landscape, and living environment must be protected, and new construction takes place within established building possibilities and zoning plans.

124546.explanation: Sports clubs receive a municipal contribution if members have benefits, so that sports remain affordable for everyone.

124548.explanation: Our party believes every child should be able to take music lessons. Subsidies are channeled through local music associations to ensure quality, local involvement, and broad participation.

124551.explanation: Our party considers the deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs) important for safety and law enforcement in the municipality. A targeted and efficient deployment is central: capacity is used where social added value is greatest, so residents and entrepreneurs genuinely benefit.

124552.explanation: Camera surveillance is only permitted if it increases safety or is strictly necessary, with residents' privacy always guaranteed.

124556.explanation: Our party has long believed that permanent residence in vacation homes should be possible. This offers residents more housing, supports movement in the housing market, and contributes to livability in our municipality.

124557.explanation: Build only within village boundaries, unless approved by zoning plans; this preserves our landscape and village character.

124558.explanation: Our party believes that people receiving social assistance can perform socially meaningful work to increase involvement and self-reliance. The municipality can stimulate and facilitate this, but cannot make it mandatory. We would consider it desirable.

124560.explanation: Aid for poverty: a single national income standard prevents inequality between municipalities.

124562.explanation: Charging stations are important in the startup phase of electric driving, but expansion is not a municipal task. Charging electric cars should increasingly take place on private property, making residents and businesses responsible for their own charging facilities.

124563.explanation: Our party wants to lower the maximum speed within built-up areas to 30 km/h to increase traffic safety and keep streets livable. Fatbikes are unwanted in built-up areas because they may reduce the safety of other road users.

124564.explanation: Our party believes that people needing care should have the option to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs. This must be done responsibly, ensuring quality, continuity, and affordability of care.

124565.explanation: Youth care is jointly purchased with fixed standards for price and quality. Cooperation between organizations guarantees continuity and good care for young people.

124567.explanation: Development of industrial estates in Tholen must match actual demand, prioritizing local entrepreneurs and SMEs. This stimulates the economy without unnecessary expansion or vacancy.

124568.explanation: We prefer to leave shopping Sundays to entrepreneurs themselves, with concern for peace and livability. If an entrepreneur wants it, it should be possible. However, all delivery services remain active on Sundays, which may cause unfair competition.

124574.explanation: Our party believes attention to the Zeeland dialect in primary schools is unnecessary; children naturally learn the language in their environment.

124575.explanation: Our party is against a coffeeshop in Tholen and advocates more focus on prevention and support for hard drug use.

124576.explanation: The share of social rental homes in new construction in Tholen must align with local needs. No social rental homes will be built for residents of other municipalities; our own residents come first.

124577.explanation: No high-rise residential buildings in Tholen: high-rise does not fit the rural character of our villages.

124578.explanation: Landlords may rent out individual rooms, provided this fits the zoning plan and is done safely and responsibly.

124579.explanation: Our party believes that parking in town centers must be properly regulated according to the established standards in the ordinance. Additional parking spaces may only be created if they contribute to the accessibility of shops and facilities, without compromising the livability and tranquility of the streets.

124581.explanation: Our party believes that the number of aldermen should correspond to the municipal tasks. Additional aldermen are only necessary for effective policy implementation.

124583.explanation: Tholen must realize recreational facilities, such as public toilets, more quickly. These should be aligned with tourist and local recreational needs and available in all towns. Especially given the decline in hospitality venues, there is a greater need for such facilities.

124530.explanation: Representatives are elected and make decisions without instructions or interference. However, our party is indeed in favor of citizen participation.

124534.explanation: ChristenUnie supports sustainable energy. Additionally, energy coaches facilitate this. Another example is Tholen Solar.

124536.explanation: Tholen has always supported customized shelter and will take responsibility for social challenges. The municipality actively contributes to the tasks imposed by the government. Therefore, we do not opt for large-scale asylum seeker centers.

124541.explanation: Unless a citizen initiative arises from the community, then we facilitate it.

124542.explanation: Currently a fee is charged for this, but the recycling center is free of charge.

124544.explanation: Yes, but then with an advisory role. For the municipality itself, it is a spearhead.

124545.explanation: No cluttering of the rural area.

124548.explanation: Tholen is reconsidering support for families regarding music lessons. Tholen is withdrawing from the Zeeland Music School. A new offering still needs to be developed. We see opportunities for music associations in Tholen combined with a potential personal music budget.

124551.explanation: Focus on connecting and strengthening society and tackling undermining of the countryside.

124552.explanation: No to camera surveillance. More APR cameras on access roads for serious crime.

124557.explanation: Infill development offers insufficient solution; every town may grow. However, this must be integrated and without cluttering the countryside.

124558.explanation: Effort may be required. Everyone participates.

124562.explanation: More charging facilities are needed, which is somewhat different from designating parking spaces.

124564.explanation: ChristenUnie stands for appropriate and also identity-based care when clients desire it.

124565.explanation: Providers are not selected based on price; the price is determined in advance through cost price research, so quality is taken into account.

124567.explanation: Tholen's business park may grow; other towns allow small-scale growth.

124574.explanation: It is important to strengthen the uniqueness and identity of young people.

124575.explanation: From the ChristenUnie's perspective, this does not belong in our society.

124577.explanation: No new plans on top of existing ones.

124578.explanation: We want to counteract nuisance and abuse. Since we are not a student city, we focus on renting homes to families. Exploitative renting must be opposed.

124579.explanation: An important part of the mobility vision.

124581.explanation: It is first up to the voter and for ChristenUnie not a fixed position. It is a consequence of the coalition to be formed.

124583.explanation: Make this part of the tourism development plan.

124530.explanation: CDA Tholen considers citizen participation important. We support the use of a referendum as an advisory tool to gauge public opinion, but not as a binding decision-making method. Decision-making is the responsibility of the elected representatives.

124533.explanation: We want to give farmers space to diversify agricultural activities (e.g., recreation, home sales, agro-tourism) without hindering primary production. All within the countryside and certain frameworks where quality takes precedence over quantity.

124534.explanation: Support should mainly consist of facilitating and thus supporting such initiatives. We support financial aid to residents who jointly generate sustainable energy, provided this is done transparently and lawfully.

124536.explanation: We are against large-scale or permanent shelter locations. Temporary, humane shelter for people in need is negotiable within the capacity of the local community and in cooperation with the region.

124541.explanation: A municipal fireworks display aligns with the CDA vision to preserve traditions safely. This can be achieved through cooperation between the municipality, associations, and entrepreneurs as a way to realize a festive New Year's Eve without nuisance.

124542.explanation: Free collection of bulky waste at home may be desirable for vulnerable groups but should not become a structural policy. Abuse, high costs, and convenience are risks. We focus on good accessibility and continued free use of the recycling center.

124544.explanation: We support financial assistance to make buildings more accessible for people with disabilities. We want the municipality not only to set rules but also to actively help with subsidies, advice, and cooperation.

124545.explanation: Housing construction for local needs with room for customization. Small-scale housing on agricultural estates provided that: it fits within the local housing vision, it does not lead to uncontrolled urbanization, landscape and ecological values are preserved, and no restrictions apply to nearby farmers.

124546.explanation: We opt for customization and support: for example through special assistance, municipal schemes, or cooperation with sports clubs to reduce/keep membership fees affordable.

124548.explanation: We support municipal subsidies for music lessons for primary school children, provided that: the policy is transparent and feasible, there is cooperation with schools and associations, and it contributes to equal opportunities and talent development.

124551.explanation: We want more visible enforcement and thus safety in towns and rural areas. Extra enforcement officers, night patrols, and cooperation with farmers, hunters, and land managers are necessary due to GPS thefts, roaming gangs, and drug labs in the countryside.

124552.explanation: Privacy is important, but preventing conflicts or vandalism/theft of (public) property is also important. Camera surveillance can be a good solution in those cases, and we apply it.

124556.explanation: We focus on sufficient housing for permanent residents within villages. Permanent residence in holiday parks can put pressure on facilities and lead to loss of the tourist character. CDA Tholen wants to actively manage a balanced housing supply, not including holiday parks.

124557.explanation: We want to build as much as possible within village boundaries to protect the landscape, nature, and (agricultural) activities outside the centers. Preference for infill and restructuring; new construction outside centers only if there are no alternatives and it fits within spatial planning vision.

124558.explanation: CDA Tholen supports a reciprocal obligation with social assistance. Socially meaningful work increases engagement and self-worth, provided individual capacities are taken into account.

124560.explanation: We want more residents to be helped faster and better with poverty. A higher income limit can ensure that more residents, including low-income workers, receive assistance.

124562.explanation: We want more charging facilities for electric cars. However, this must be combined with addressing parking problems throughout the entire municipality of Tholen.

124563.explanation: A maximum speed limit of 30 km/h will become the standard within all built-up areas and a maximum of 15 km/h on residential streets. However, this does not apply to so-called Area Access Roads (through-roads); speed there remains 50 km/h for accessibility and traffic flow.

124564.explanation: We support this freedom of choice in healthcare: Reasoning: Respect for diversity and personal beliefs. Care should not only be of good quality but also fit with the client’s identity.

124565.explanation: When choosing organizations for youth care, quality, continuity, and appropriate care must come first, not just the lowest price.

124567.explanation: We want to invest in accessible, safe, and future-proof (local) business parks. The emphasis is on more space for local entrepreneurs on local business sites.

124568.explanation: We emphasize the importance of rest on Sundays and preserving Sunday rest as part of local culture and livability. At the same time, it gives residents and entrepreneurs (especially retailers) the opportunity to enjoy this (day off) themselves.

124574.explanation: We support attention for the Zeeland dialect. As CDA Tholen, we show respect for our values and our cultural, religious, agricultural, and historical heritage, as well as for the Zeeland dialect and the cultural festivals within our communities. We want to preserve this heritage for future generations.

124575.explanation: We are against establishing a coffee shop. We oppose promoting drug use and trade and believe the municipality should focus on prevention and enforcement.

124576.explanation: We join the Zeeland Housing Deal. In this context, Tholen aims for about 495 social rental homes by 2030, which amounts to approximately 30% of the total housing target for the coming years.

124577.explanation: We do not want high-rise apartment buildings. We believe Tholen should maintain a village and rural appearance. Limited mid-rise buildings of a few floors may occur locally when they fit the existing buildings and have support from local residents.

124578.explanation: CDA Tholen is positive about room rental as part of a broader strategy for affordable housing, provided there are clear rules. This aligns with our commitment to more housing and flexibility.

124579.explanation: We want sufficient parking facilities in the centers, especially near housing developments and village centers, and see this as part of a broader mobility and livability strategy.

124581.explanation: We find it important that an organization like a municipality has sufficient capacity to carry out complex tasks. This may mean we are open to expansion if needed to ensure quality and effectiveness.

124583.explanation: We support realizing recreational facilities such as public toilets. This fits within our vision of livability, tourism, and social cohesion.

124530.explanation: In our country, we elect representatives who formulate policy on our behalf. This usually works well. However, we want to organize a regular opportunity in every village where residents can engage in dialogue with elected representatives.

124533.explanation: Farmers are forced by national policy and market forces to diversify their sources of income. Naturally, this must happen within existing regulations such as hygiene, etc.

124534.explanation: This would fit into the national policy of making our energy sector sustainable. Municipal support does not have to be a grant; it could also consist of a loan and/or administrative support.

124536.explanation: This would be in accordance with applicable legislation (distribution law).

124541.explanation: From 2026, the sale of fireworks to private individuals will probably be banned. Then a municipal-organized show would be a good alternative.

124542.explanation: People with a low income hardly generate bulky waste. People doing renovations, which produce a lot of bulky waste, can factor disposal costs into their renovation expenses.

124544.explanation: This is the responsibility of entrepreneurs and is also legally arranged as such. However, the municipality is responsible for accessibility in the public domain, for example well-designed and well-maintained sidewalks.

124545.explanation: To some extent: for example, a business takeover by a son or daughter while the parents want to remain living on the farm, or operation of the business by a partnership of multiple persons who want to live on the farm with their families.

124546.explanation: Even better would be to increase social assistance benefits so that all additional schemes become unnecessary, but that is up to national politics. Sport is very important for people's health: in the long run, such schemes pay for themselves at least partly.

124548.explanation: Even more so: every primary school child should be able to get good music education at school from qualified teachers. Our education currently offers far too little in the cultural subjects!

124551.explanation: Tholen is not an unsafe municipality. There is crime, which must be combated by the police, not by enforcement officers. Much minor (juvenile) crime could be prevented by better youth policies, for example by more youth and young people’s work, youth centers, etc.

124552.explanation: Tholen is a sparsely populated municipality: where should cameras be placed and where not? And who should review all those camera images?

124556.explanation: But within limitations. For example, there could be a maximum number of years that a recreational home can be permanently inhabited. It is also conceivable that one park is more or less suitable for this than another. Good consultation with all involved is necessary.

124557.explanation: It is unavoidable that communities must expand beyond existing village boundaries.

124558.explanation: The majority of people on social assistance would like to fully participate in society again, including paid work. Good guidance to achieve this is certainly necessary. Coercion is undesirable and unproductive.

124560.explanation: The limit is currently set at 130%; that seems a realistic figure to us.

124562.explanation: When residents purchase an electric car, they can request the installation of a public charging station from the municipality (or have a charging station installed on their own property). This system works properly.

124563.explanation: Where necessary, yes. In other places, it is not required.

124564.explanation: In healthcare provision, the focus is on the quality of care offered, not on the caregiver's personal beliefs.

124565.explanation: The primary concern is the quality and accessibility of help.

124567.explanation: Preferably not, but if necessary for employment purposes, it must be open for discussion.

124568.explanation: Most shops and small businesses, such as in hospitality, are family-run. They also have the right to a stable family life. And a complete Sunday closure of shops is also quite clear.

124574.explanation: Zeelandic dialect is of course not a language like Frisian. But it is conceivable that it could be offered as an optional subject in primary schools.

124575.explanation: If properly regulated, a coffeeshop can prevent illegal drug trade and thus reduce greater criminal influence.

124576.explanation: Not really a point of controversy.

124577.explanation: Up to, for example, five residential floors is conceivable, provided it is within town centers. Not in the form of a large hotel by the Oosterschelde.

124578.explanation: This attracts unscrupulous landlords practices, whose main victims are labor migrants.

124579.explanation: Public transport is rapidly deteriorating in quality. So residents become increasingly dependent on their own car. They must then be able to park near their home.

124581.explanation: A few years ago there were four aldermen. That has changed to three, and so far that appears sufficient in practice.

124583.explanation: But the municipality has already decided that themselves.

124530.explanation: Not necessarily a referendum; the participation process is especially important!

124533.explanation: Not always; conditions may be imposed, e.g., hygiene requirements. It must also fit the environment and not cause (noise) nuisance.

124536.explanation: We leave it to our legal obligations.

124541.explanation: In our opinion, the municipality has no responsibility here and it would involve high costs to do this in every center. An unnecessary burden on the environment.

124542.explanation: Residents can take it to the landfill for free. If it must be collected, it is fair that the municipality charges a fee (currently €31) and sets conditions on weight etc.

124544.explanation: Some form of incentivizing is fine. In the context of, among others, occupational health and safety, employers (thus also entrepreneurs) already have to arrange certain things.

124551.explanation: At the time a motion was submitted requesting double the number of positions. It was rejected by the council. The upcoming evaluation will undoubtedly result in more FTE BOA (enforcement officers).

124552.explanation: Disagree; there are no seriously nuisance-causing areas.

124556.explanation: Preventing recreation parks from deteriorating and removing recreational homes from the market.

124557.explanation: If fitting within the environmental plan, a village adjoining the village border may expand.

124558.explanation: No obligation; there are people for whom it is absolutely not possible. Each situation can be examined whether someone can contribute, and if necessary, motivated. The choice lies with the individual involved. Living on welfare is no picnic.

124560.explanation: Already at 130% of the minimum wage.

124562.explanation: There is a shortage of this, especially regarding electric company vans.

124563.explanation: Already in place; main roads within built-up areas have a 50 km/h speed limit.

124564.explanation: No debate needed. Freedom of choice in care provider must remain.

124565.explanation: What matters most is the quality offered and whether the intended care is appropriate.

124567.explanation: In principle, initially expansion only for own entrepreneurs.

124568.explanation: The entrepreneur should be able to decide for themselves whether they want to be open on Sunday or not.

124574.explanation: Not necessary to maintain culture there.

124575.explanation: Does not fit well with Tholen's culture and risks causing nuisance.

124576.explanation: Given the shortage of social housing, urgently necessary.

124577.explanation: Not fitting in the landscape: a maximum of three floors seems sufficient.

124578.explanation: Provided that laws, regulations, and the General Local Regulation (APV) are complied with.

124579.explanation: Sufficient parking facilities on the edge of a town center.

124581.explanation: With 3 FTE it is manageable; if the workload is perceived as too high, 4 times 0.8 FTE might be a good solution.

124583.explanation: It is indeed urgently needed due to the growth of (day) tourism.

124530.explanation: Citizen participation is very important! The municipality exists to listen to its residents and to align its policies with them as much as possible. A referendum is a good means to express this. Whether a referendum is suitable depends on the subject.

124533.explanation: We agree under several strict conditions: provided the setup is sustainable, respects nature, and is not close to vulnerable natural areas. It leads to more traffic and puts pressure on the surroundings. But it also contributes to liveliness and opportunities to experience the environment.

124534.explanation: Residents with beautiful, sustainable initiatives deserve financial support from the municipality. And not to forget support in insulating houses as well. This reduces the need to generate energy and helps prevent energy poverty. Everyone should be able to switch to affordable sustainable energy!

124536.explanation: Refugees must be offered shelter: people who have left their home, house, family, friends, and country behind. Shelter should be balanced with the size of the village or city where the center is located. Compassion focused on refugees from unsafe countries who really need it.

124541.explanation: Fireworks cause nuisance for people and animals and are not good for the environment. The municipality can consider alternative celebrations for New Year's Eve for its residents.

124542.explanation: Responsible waste processing costs a lot of money. Ultimately, we must move towards a circular economy in which waste is reused and the waste stream is continuously reduced. Free waste collection encourages producing more waste instead of dealing with it consciously.

124544.explanation: We stand for an inclusive society in which everyone should be able to participate. Easily accessible buildings are part of that. People should be able to enter and exit these buildings independently. The municipality must support this for its residents.

124545.explanation: No, because we strive for landscape quality without fragmented uncontrolled growth of houses in the countryside and across the island.

124546.explanation: The municipality has a duty of care for the welfare of residents with a low income. Especially children living in poverty must be able to participate in sports. The return of school swimming is also very important. Physical activity is healthy. A healthy lifestyle ultimately also saves money!

124548.explanation: We would like to see music lessons become a structural part of the curriculum in primary schools.

124551.explanation: More BOS officers means better enforcement of laws and regulations and support for the police. In the rural area, sufficient supervision is lacking, causing environmental offenses such as illegal dumping and entering vulnerable nature areas to go unnoticed.

124552.explanation: We oppose more camera surveillance simply for the sake of more cameras. Privacy is a high value. However, if there is reason to place a new camera somewhere because of an unsafe situation or risk, this is fine with proper justification.

124556.explanation: Holiday homes are not suitable as permanent residences: they cause traffic, waste, permanent residents who heat a lot because these houses are often poorly insulated, and holiday homes lead to fragmented habitation over the entire island.

124557.explanation: The preference is for infilling and connecting to existing village boundaries to preserve landscape quality.

124558.explanation: It would be good if everyone occasionally does socially meaningful work. It may be that people are already active as caregivers or volunteers. Obliging is not the right approach; motivating and offering opportunities is a better strategy.

124560.explanation: The income limit used to receive help for poverty is sufficient and does not need to be raised.

124562.explanation: The existing mobility vision promotes electric driving and requires suppliers to the municipality, such as passenger transport vans, to provide electric transport. This is feasible if there are sufficient charging facilities and is the municipality's responsibility.

124563.explanation: Indeed, this greatly benefits the safety of vulnerable road users, pedestrians, and cyclists. Playing children don't always pay full attention and accidents can be better prevented at lower speeds. Speeding often only saves a few minutes.

124564.explanation: People must be treated with respect, taking beliefs into account. Care can be provided to each other despite differing convictions. Seeking connection with each other, including in care, is beautiful and positive. We must guard against only people with the same beliefs meeting each other.

124565.explanation: No, the municipality should mainly focus on quality, professionalism, and tailor-made solutions. This pays for itself. Looking purely at money leads to poorer help and fewer problems being solved, which ultimately results in higher costs.

124567.explanation: Connecting to existing business parks, where necessary infrastructure is already present, is very feasible.

124568.explanation: Although we are also in favor of slowing down and a quiet Sunday can be nice, it should remain an individual choice. We prefer to leave it to entrepreneurs and individual residents to determine how Sunday is spent.

124574.explanation: We are positive about such an initiative on a voluntary basis. It is an expression of own culture. We prefer that schools themselves pay attention to this and that only occasional project fees are requested. So, no structural support.

124575.explanation: There is no need for a coffeeshop in Tholen, and residents are concerned about nuisance and regional attraction. There are already enough coffeeshops in surrounding municipalities.

124576.explanation: Tholen is proud to be among the cheapest municipalities and that must remain. Also regarding a sufficient number of social rental homes! Not everyone can afford to buy a home nowadays, even dual earners struggle to pay.

124577.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit in Tholen's rural environment. They harm the characteristic spaciousness of Tholen. More homes must be built, but ones that fit Tholen.

124578.explanation: We are positive about renting rooms as part of the solution to the housing crisis, especially when it concerns shared housing with good protection for tenants.

124579.explanation: Because there is no space in the narrow streets of the villages. Increasing parking in the villages jeopardizes accessibility for emergency services. Creating more parking at the edges of villages also improves traffic safety.

124581.explanation: As more accountability is transferred from the national government to the municipality, the number of issues and major files grows to such an extent that it would be good for workload and work distribution.

124583.explanation: Our party was the first to put public toilets on the agenda in our municipality. Chronically ill people cannot do without them, and there were no public toilets in Tholen. That has changed now and expansion must continue.

statements.title: Tholen must implement recreational facilities, such as public toilets, at a faster pace.

titleParts.text: Tholen moet in een hoger tempo recreatieve

titleParts.glossary: Buildings or services needed for daily life, such as roads, sports facilities or healthcare.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The residents of Tholen should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Tholen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Tholen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Tholen should get more business/industrial parks.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Tholen should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Zeeuws dialect.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
At least 30% of the new homes built in Tholen should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Tholen moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
High-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Tholen.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: After the elections there should be a fourth alderman (wethouder).
